channel.title: Tyler's Treks
channel.description: Exploring every California national park in 2026, camping out of a truck.

item.title: December 11, 2025 — White Cross World War I Memorial, Nipton, California
item.description: The first night in the camper shell! I drove up to Nipton, CA and camped out right next to [White Cross World War I Memorial](https://maps.app.goo.gl/q6prwbHUXk6rEk8L7). The spot was perfect for my first time, plenty of free camping spots and some great trails nearby.

item.title: December 13, 2025 — Hole-in-the-Wall Campground, Essex, California
item.description: Second outing was definitely lazier in the setup. After getting cleaned out by Vegas, I drove up to Essex, CA and camped out near [Hole in the Wall Campground](https://maps.app.goo.gl/atCuCDzLRG7YN7HE8). By the time I got there, I was cooked so I just laid in my sleeping bag and used my sweatshirts as a pillow.

item.title: January 1, 2026 — Pinnacle Road, Trona, California
item.description: Happy New Year! Drove out 3 hours around sunset to Trona Pinnacles. I should have left a wee bit earlier as it was tough to navigate in the dark. I stopped about 5 miles short on a random dirt road off of Pinnacle Road. My truck felt the effect of the rain on the dirt roads these past couple weeks.

item.title: January 2, 2026 — Trona and Ridgecrest, California
item.description: Oh boy. Buckle up for this one. Woke up at 6 am because I was worrying about the mud pit I had driven through the night before. I wanted to just get through it as early as possible in case anything went wrong. And in fact, everything went wrong. I tried to be sneaky and cut around the water that I barely made it through the night before and ended up getting stuck in a mud pit. I did everything you aren't supposed to do when you're in mud. I floored it in drive, floored it in reverse, turned my tires every which way. After I had dug myself deeper in the mud pit, I found some wood left by others who had also gotten stuck but were able to get out. I tried shimmying those under my tires and giving it another go. Did it work? Absolutely not.

item.title: January 3, 2026 — Death Valley National Park, California
item.description: Today was the big hiking day. First hike of the day was [Mosaic Canyon](https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/mosaic-canyon-trail). Very beautiful, like being on the moon with the black gravel. Second hike was [Golden Canyon](https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/golden-canyon-trail-and-badlands-loop-to-zabriskie-point). This one was the bee's knees. Rock slabs were incredible. The last hike of the day was the [Mesquite Flat Sand Dunes](https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/mesquite-flat-sand-dunes-trail) right before sunset.

item.title: January 4, 2026 — Badwater Road, Death Valley National Park, California
item.description: Wrapped up the trip with a drive down Badwater Road to see [Badwater Basin](https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/badwater-basin-salt-flats-trail), Lake Manly, and [Artist's Palette](https://www.alltrails.com/explore/poi/us/california/death-valley/artists-palette).

item.title: May 8, 2026 — Black Rock Canyon Campground, Joshua Tree National Park, California
May 9, 2026 — Hidden Valley Trail, Joshua Tree National Park, California
May 17, 2026 — Sulfur Works & Mill Creek Resort, Lassen Volcanic National Park, California
May 18, 2026 — Mill Creek Falls Trail & Highway 89, Lassen Volcanic, California
May 19, 2026 — Mill Creek Trail, Stout Grove & Grove of Titans — Jedediah Smith Redwoods, California
May 20, 2026 — James Irvine Trail, Fern Canyon & Simpson-Reed Trail — Prairie Creek Redwoods, California
May 21, 2026 — Ten Taypo–Hope Creek Loop & Avenue of Giants, Humboldt Redwoods, California
